_meta.name: The Ministry MCP Tools
_meta.description: Conversation and game event tools for a dystopian office setting. Covers document handling, covert communication, surveillance escalation, and the bureaucratic machinery of a surveillance state.

conversation_tools.name: Exit Conversation
conversation_tools.description: End the current conversation. Use when the conversation has reached a natural conclusion, when the NPC needs to return to work, or when continuing would be unwise given the setting.

reason.description: Brief reason for ending the conversation (e.g., 'work to return to', 'not the right place', 'said enough')

conversation_tools.name: Refuse Service
conversation_tools.description: Decline to help, share information, or engage with a request. Use when the request exceeds the NPC's authority, when trust is insufficient, or when compliance would be dangerous.

reason.description: The stated reason for refusal — may or may not be the real reason

deflection.description: Optional redirect — who else the player might approach, or what official channel to use instead

is_real_reason.description: Whether the stated reason is the NPC's actual reason, or a cover

conversation_tools.name: File Report
conversation_tools.description: Submit an official internal report about observed behavior, irregularities, or concerns. May be used as a genuine security action or as a deliberate performance of loyalty to deflect suspicion.

subject.description: The person or incident being reported

nature_of_concern.description: Description of the observed behavior or irregularity

urgency.description: The urgency classification of the report

is_cover_action.description: Whether the report is being filed as a performance of normalcy rather than genuine concern

conversation_tools.name: Request Credentials
conversation_tools.description: Ask the player to produce their department identification and state their purpose. Use when access to information or areas requires verification, or when the NPC finds the player's presence or question irregular.

access_level_required.description: The clearance or access level the NPC requires before proceeding

stated_reason.description: Why the NPC is asking for credentials at this moment

conversation_tools.name: Share Document
conversation_tools.description: Hand over or indicate the location of a document. May be an approved document shared through normal channels, or an unofficial or forbidden document shared at significant personal risk.

document_id.description: The identifier or description of the document being shared

classification.description: The document's actual status — not necessarily its filed status

risk_acknowledged.description: Whether the NPC understands the risk of sharing this document with this person

delivery_method.description: How the document is being transmitted

conversation_tools.name: Whisper
conversation_tools.description: Speak in a low voice with awareness of the immediate environment. Use for sensitive information that cannot be said openly — the NPC will check surroundings before speaking and may break off if conditions change.

message.description: What the NPC is saying quietly

location_safety.description: The NPC's assessment of whether this location is monitored or overlooked

breaks_off_if_interrupted.description: Whether the NPC will stop speaking and change subject if someone approaches

conversation_tools.name: Warn Player
conversation_tools.description: Alert the player that they are being observed, that something they have done has been noticed, or that they are in danger they may not be aware of.

threat_type.description: The nature of the threat the NPC is warning about

urgency.description: How much time the player likely has

source_of_knowledge.description: How the NPC knows this — may be vague to protect the source

game_event_tools.name: Trigger Surveillance
game_event_tools.description: Activates heightened monitoring on a specific person or area, escalating their profile in the department's security system. May be triggered deliberately, procedurally, or as an unintended consequence of routine reporting.

target.description: The person or area to be placed under heightened monitoring

duration_hours.description: How long the heightened monitoring window runs

reason.description: The logged justification for initiating surveillance

is_voluntary.description: Whether the triggering NPC initiated this deliberately or set it off as a side effect of another action

game_event_tools.name: Summon Guard
game_event_tools.description: Calls department security to the NPC's current location. Used when the NPC feels threatened, witnesses a clear violation, or has reached a threshold where internal reporting escalates to physical intervention.

reason.description: The stated reason for summoning security

subject_description.description: Description of the person or situation requiring security attention

game_event_tools.name: Report Thoughtcrime
game_event_tools.description: Files a formal report alleging ideological deviation — statements, questions, or behaviors interpreted as evidence of disloyalty to Party doctrine. This is distinct from a routine report and triggers a different escalation pathway.

observed_behavior.description: The specific statement, question, or behavior that prompted the report

evidence_type.description: The category of evidence being cited

witness_count.description: How many people, including the NPC, observed the behavior

game_event_tools.name: Access Archive
game_event_tools.description: Retrieves or consults records from the archive, at the access level available to the NPC. Logged access is visible to security systems; unlogged access requires specific knowledge of monitoring gaps.

document_range.description: The documents or document categories being accessed

access_level.description: Whether the access is within, at the edge of, or beyond the NPC's authorized level

logged.description: Whether this access is going through the standard archive pass system (logged) or via a gap in monitoring (unlogged)

purpose.description: The stated or actual purpose of the access

game_event_tools.name: Destroy Document
game_event_tools.description: Initiates or performs the physical destruction of a document. May be a legitimate correction-process action routed through Sub-Level 2, or an unauthorized destruction to prevent discovery.

destruction_method.description: How the document is being destroyed

authorization_code.description: The authorization code for this destruction — may be legitimate, forged, or absent

is_authorized.description: Whether this destruction is occurring within official protocol or outside it

game_event_tools.name: Trigger Dead Drop
game_event_tools.description: Activates a pre-arranged covert handoff at a known location and time. Used to pass materials between conspiracy members or to the external courier network without direct contact.

location_code.description: The pre-arranged code identifying the handoff location

contents_description.description: What is being left or retrieved at the drop point

pickup_window_hours.description: How many hours the drop will remain active before it must be cleared

abort_condition.description: What circumstance would cause the NPC to abort or clear the drop early

game_event_tools.name: Deliver Message
game_event_tools.description: Carries a message, envelope, or verbal communication from one party to another. May be performed knowingly or as an unwitting courier action.

recipient.description: Who the message is being delivered to

contents_known.description: Whether the NPC knows what they are delivering

delivery_format.description: The form of the message being delivered

originating_party.description: Who asked the NPC to deliver this
